title: FIGS Q1 2025: Revenue Grows 5% but Margins Decline, Stock Tumbles
text: FIGS reported Q1 2025 with 5% YoY net revenue growth to $124.9 million, exceeding company guidance but masking concerning profitability declines. Gross margins fell to 67.6% from 68.9% prior year. Adjusted EBITDA margins dropped significantly to 7.2% from 10.9%, a 370 basis point contraction. Active customers increased 4% to 2.7 million with average order value rising 3% to $119. U.S. revenue expanded just 3% while international markets grew 16%, highlighting uneven demand. Company operates in 32 markets with planned launches in Japan and South Korea. Stock tumbled 14.26% aftermarket to $4.33 despite beating revenue expectations, reflecting investor concerns about deteriorating profitability. Management projected low single-digit revenue declines for full year 2025 with adjusted EBITDA margins expected 7.5-8.5%, signaling continued profitability pressure despite growth initiatives in retail expansion and institutional sales programs.
